_metadata.description: Verified U.S. state-by-state vehicle purchase fee reference data for Odigos analyzer and content pages. All data sourced from state DMVs, departments of revenue, legislative records, and authoritative automotive sites.
notes: docFeeCap: null means no state-imposed cap
docFeeCapAmount: dollar amount if capped; null if uncapped
docFeeTypicalRange: [low, high] based on reported dealer practices
stateSalesTaxRate: base state rate as decimal (0.0625 = 6.25%)
maxCombinedTaxRate: highest possible state + local combined rate
tradeInTaxCredit: whether state allows trade-in value to reduce taxable amount
All dollar amounts in USD. All rates as decimals.
IMPORTANT: This data should be reviewed quarterly. State legislatures frequently adjust caps and rates.
confidence: 'high' = verified against primary statute/bulletin, 'medium' = verified against secondary sources, 'low' = needs human spot-check
verificationMethod: describes how the data was verified

AL.name: Alabama
AL.vehicleTaxNotes: 4% state + up to 7% local. Varies significantly by jurisdiction.
AL.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge state guide + AL Revenue dept rate table

AK.name: Alaska
AK.vehicleTaxNotes: No state sales tax. Municipalities may impose 0%-9.5%. Lithia Motors fined $300K in Dec 2025 for hidden doc fees.
AK.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: No state sales tax; municipal tax treatment varies
AK.specialNotes: Dec 2025: Lithia Motors ordered to pay $300K civil penalty for hidden doc fees. Dealers must include doc fees in advertised price.
AK.verificationMethod: Alaska AG press release (Dec 2025 Lithia Motors) + no direct fee regulation research. Unusual market.

AZ.name: Arizona
AZ.vehicleTaxNotes: 5.6% Transaction Privilege Tax + local up to 5.6%. Phoenix ~8.6%.
AZ.registrationFee: $8 base + Vehicle License Tax based on value
AZ.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + AZ DOR Transaction Privilege Tax table

AR.name: Arkansas
AR.vehicleTaxNotes: 6.5% for vehicles $10K+; 3.5% for $4K-$10K; 0% under $4K. Local additions up to 5%.
AR.specialNotes: Called 'service and handling' fees. Tiered sales tax based on vehicle price.
AR.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + AR DFA tax rate page. Cap amount needs statute verification.

CA.name: California
CA.vehicleTaxNotes: 7.25% state base + local up to 3.5%. LA 10.25%, San Diego 7.75%.
CA.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: California does NOT allow trade-in deduction. Tax applies to full vehicle price.
CA.specialNotes: $85 for DMV private industry partners, $70 for other dealers. SB 791 (raise to $260) vetoed by Governor Newsom Oct 2025.
CA.verificationMethod: Primary: CA DMV fee schedule + SB 791 veto coverage (Oct 2025)

CO.name: Colorado
CO.vehicleTaxNotes: 2.9% state + local up to ~5%. Called 'dealer handling fee.'
CO.registrationFee: Varies by weight, age, and value
CO.specialNotes: Lien filing fee increased to $40 flat rate effective July 1, 2025.
CO.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + CO tax.colorado.gov. No cap claim needs verification.

CT.name: Connecticut
CT.vehicleTaxNotes: 6.35% for vehicles under $50K; 7.75% for vehicles $50K+. No local additions.
CT.specialNotes: Multiple bills proposed in 2025 (HB 06544, HB 06827, SB 774) to cap at 1% of vehicle price. Not enacted as of March 2026.
CT.verificationMethod: Secondary: CT DMV calculator. No-cap status verified via pending bill research (HB 06544 not enacted).

DE.name: Delaware
DE.vehicleTaxNotes: 0% sales tax. 5.25% Document Fee applies instead (increased from 4.25% effective Oct 1, 2025). HB 295 proposes $475 dealer doc fee cap (pending).
DE.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Trade-in reduces taxable amount for dealer purchases only
DE.specialNotes: No sales tax but 5.25% state 'document fee' on vehicle purchases (increased Oct 2025 from 4.25%).
DE.verificationMethod: Secondary: Delaware DMV news release (Oct 2025 fee increase). HB 295 cap pending.

FL.name: Florida
FL.vehicleTaxNotes: 6% state. County surtax 0.5%-2.5% applies only to first $5,000 of purchase price.
FL.specialNotes: Florida has some of the highest doc fees in the nation. No cap exists despite multiple legislative attempts.
FL.verificationMethod: Primary: Florida Revenue statutes + etags.com dealer analysis

GA.name: Georgia
GA.vehicleTaxNotes: 4% state + local average 3.49%. Georgia also charges Title Ad Valorem Tax (TAVT) at 7.0% of fair market value (one-time, at titling). TAVT replaces annual ad valorem tax.
GA.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: TAVT calculated on net price after trade-in deduction (dealer sales only)
GA.specialNotes: TAVT at 7.0% of fair market value is a one-time tax paid at titling. Must be included in advertised price per AG guidance.
GA.verificationMethod: Primary: Georgia DOR TAVT FAQ + AG consumer guidance

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.vehicleTaxNotes: 4% General Excise Tax + 0.5% county surcharge. Private-party purchases exempt.
HI.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: No deduction for sales tax. Use tax on imported vehicles allows trade-in reduction.
HI.specialNotes: Luxury vehicle surcharge: 50% of applicable taxes (new legislation).
HI.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + Hawaii GET rate page. Trade-in credit status needs verification.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Idaho DOES allow trade-in deductions per IDAPA 35.01.02.044.
ID.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + Idaho tax guide. Trade-in credit status corrected to true per IDAPA.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.vehicleTaxNotes: 6.25% state + local/RTA up to 4%. Chicago combined ~10.25%.
IL.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: UNLIMITED as of Jan 1, 2022 (Public Act 102-0353 removed $10K cap).
IL.specialNotes: Cap adjusts annually by CPI. Modern cap started at $300 effective Jan 1, 2020. For 2025: $367.70. For 2026: $377.63. Increases ~2-3%/year.
IL.verificationMethod: Primary: Illinois Automobile Dealers Assoc. + ILSOS fee schedule

IN.name: Indiana
IN.specialNotes: Cap adjusted annually by CPI. Must be included in advertised price and disclosed separately on bill of sale. Effective July 1, 2025: $251.05.
IN.verificationMethod: Primary: Indiana SOS Auto Dealer Services Division guidance PDF

IA.name: Iowa
IA.vehicleTaxNotes: 5% registration fee on purchases + optional 1% Local Option Sales Tax.
IA.registrationFee: Variable by weight and list price
IA.specialNotes: Title fee increased from $25 to $35 effective Jan 1, 2025.
IA.verificationMethod: Secondary: Iowa Code §322.19A. Cap amount $180 from statute text.

KS.name: Kansas
KS.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Full deduction; expanded 120-day window as of Jan 1, 2025
KS.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + KS Revenue pub1526. No-cap claim needs verification.

KY.name: Kentucky
KY.vehicleTaxNotes: 6% Motor Vehicle Usage Tax. No local additions.
KY.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Full deduction for new vehicles; limited for used
KY.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + KY Revenue Motor Vehicle Usage Tax page

LA.name: Louisiana
LA.vehicleTaxNotes: 5% state (increased from 4.45% on Jan 1, 2025) + local up to 8.5%. Highest combined rates (Monroe/Sterlington) reach 13.5%. Among highest in nation.
LA.specialNotes: Doc fee cap $425, CPI-adjusted annually starting Jan 1, 2026. State rate increased Jan 2025.
LA.verificationMethod: Primary: HB 476 enrolled text + LA RS 6:969.18

ME.name: Maine
ME.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Trade-in must be same category (motor vehicle for motor vehicle)
ME.verificationMethod: Secondary: Maine statute §953-A + Revenue tax rate page

MD.name: Maryland
MD.vehicleTaxNotes: 6.5% excise tax (increased from 6% effective July 1, 2025). No local additions for vehicles.
MD.specialNotes: Doc fee cap increased from ~$500 to $800 effective July 1, 2024 (SB 362). Sales tax increased from 6% to 6.5% effective July 1, 2025.
MD.verificationMethod: Primary: SB 362 enrolled text + MVA Bulletin

MA.name: Massachusetts
MA.specialNotes: Title prep fee only is capped at $5 per Directive 14-1. General doc fees are uncapped.
MA.verificationMethod: Secondary: Mass Directive 14-1 (title prep only). General doc fee no-cap claim needs verification.

MI.name: Michigan
MI.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Maximum $11,000 credit or actual agreed value, whichever is less (2025)
MI.specialNotes: Cap is $280 OR 5% of vehicle price, whichever is LESS. Adjusted biennially by CPI. Per DIFS Bulletin 2025-03-CF.

MN.name: Minnesota
MN.vehicleTaxNotes: 6.875% state + up to 0.5% metro transit tax.
MN.specialNotes: MAJOR CHANGE: Cap increased from ~$125 to $350 effective July 1, 2025. Or 10% of sale value for vehicles ≤$3,499, whichever is less.

MS.name: Mississippi
MS.vehicleTaxNotes: 5% for passenger vehicles (different from 7% general sales tax). Max local addition 0.25%.
MS.specialNotes: District-based caps: fees cannot exceed 25% above district average (~$425 typical). Not a hard state cap.
MS.verificationMethod: Secondary: MMVC Regulation 8. District-based cap system is unusual — needs human review.

MO.name: Missouri
MO.vehicleTaxNotes: 4.225% state + local up to 5.875%. Max total tax capped at $725.
MO.specialNotes: Doc fee cap $500 base, increased annually by CPI. Effective Aug 17, 2025.

MT.name: Montana
MT.specialNotes: One of five states with no sales tax on vehicles (along with AK, DE, NH, OR).
MT.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge. No sales tax is well-established.

NE.name: Nebraska
NE.verificationMethod: Secondary: NE DMV registration fees page + CarEdge

NV.name: Nevada
NV.vehicleTaxNotes: 4.6% state + local varies. Reno area reportedly up to 16.89% in some contexts (verify).
NV.registrationFee: $33 base + value-based fees (35% of MSRP formula)
NV.verificationMethod: Secondary: CarEdge + NV DMV fee schedule. No-cap claim needs verification.

NH.name: New Hampshire
NH.docFeeCapNotes: State-filed paperwork fee capped at $27 (RSA 361-A:1, IV), but dealers charge separate uncapped 'administrative fees' that are NOT capped. Effective total doc fee is uncapped.
NH.specialNotes: Two-part fee structure: (1) $27 state doc fee (capped by statute), (2) Dealer admin fee (uncapped, must be itemized). Total fees average $459. Title fee increased to $37 effective Jan 1, 2026.
NH.verificationMethod: Secondary: NH Banking Dept FAQ for $27 cap. Uncapped admin fee avg from CarEdge.

NJ.name: New Jersey
NJ.verificationMethod: Secondary: NJ MVC fee schedule. No-cap claim needs verification.

NM.name: New Mexico
NM.vehicleTaxNotes: 4% motor vehicle excise tax (not traditional sales tax). Gross Receipts Tax 5.125% base + local up to 4.315%.
NM.specialNotes: New Mexico uses both a Motor Vehicle Excise Tax and a Gross Receipts Tax — verify which applies to your transaction.
NM.verificationMethod: Secondary: NM MVD + dual tax structure (excise + GRT) is complex.

NY.name: New York
NY.vehicleTaxNotes: 4% state + local varies. NYC combined up to 8.875%.
NY.registrationFee: $26-$56.50 state weight fee + $25 plate fee + county tax
NY.specialNotes: Cap set Aug 18, 2021. Still current as of March 2026.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.vehicleTaxNotes: 3% Highway Use Tax (HUT, not sales tax). Flat, no local addition.
NC.specialNotes: NC uses Highway Use Tax instead of sales tax for vehicles. Relatively low at 3%.
NC.verificationMethod: Primary: NCDOT Highway Use Tax page

ND.name: North Dakota
ND.vehicleTaxNotes: 5% excise tax (vehicle-specific). No local add-on for vehicles.
ND.registrationFee: Varies by year registered + vehicle weight
ND.verificationMethod: Secondary: ND DOT + CarEdge. Low-population state, limited data.

OH.name: Ohio
OH.registrationFee: Varies by type; service fee increased $5 to $8 effective Jan 1, 2026
OH.specialNotes: Cap is $387 OR 10% of cash price, whichever is LOWER. Title fee increase of $3 effective Jan 1, 2026.

OK.name: Oklahoma
OK.vehicleTaxNotes: 4.5% combined (1.25% sales + 3.25% excise for new vehicles). No local add-on for vehicles.

OR.name: Oregon
OR.vehicleTaxNotes: NO sales tax. 0.5% Vehicle Privilege Tax on new vehicles only. HB 3991 proposes increase to 2.25% (pending referendum).
OR.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: N/A — no sales tax. Privilege tax applies to gross sale price.
OR.registrationFee: $43 (2-year); +$60 increase for EV/40+ MPG vehicles effective Jan 1, 2026
OR.specialNotes: $250 with integrator / $200 without integrator. Title fee is sliding scale $77-$192 based on MPG.
OR.verificationMethod: Primary: ORS §822.043 confirmed $250/$200 cap. Rate: $250 with integrator.

PA.name: Pennsylvania
PA.vehicleTaxNotes: 6% state (increasing to 6.5% Oct 1, 2025 and 7% Oct 1, 2026). Philadelphia 8% (6% + 2% local).
PA.specialNotes: Cap: $490 electronic / $409 non-electronic (effective Jan 13, 2026). Adjusted annually by CPI. Up from $477/$398 in 2025.

RI.name: Rhode Island
RI.specialNotes: $400 cap applies to vehicles sold for $10,000+. Doc fee PROHIBITED on vehicles under $10,000. Separate $20 title prep fee also allowed. Increased from $200 to $400. Doc fees included in taxable measure for sales tax.
RI.verificationMethod: Secondary: 280-RICR-30-20-1.6 confirmed via LII/Cornell. $400 cap for vehicles $10K+.

SC.name: South Carolina
SC.specialNotes: Fees exceeding $225 require additional documentation to the state.
SC.verificationMethod: Secondary: SC Consumer Affairs + DOR local tax tables

SD.name: South Dakota
SD.vehicleTaxNotes: 4.2% state (reduced from 4.5% July 1, 2023) + up to 2% local. Plus 4% excise tax on purchase price.
SD.specialNotes: Among lowest doc fee averages in the nation.
SD.verificationMethod: Secondary: SD DOR fee schedule + CarEdge

TN.name: Tennessee
TN.vehicleTaxNotes: 7% state + up to 2.75% local. Memphis area reaches 9.75%.
TN.specialNotes: Among highest combined sales tax rates in the nation.
TN.verificationMethod: Secondary: TN Revenue title/registration page. High combined rates confirmed.

TX.name: Texas
TX.specialNotes: MAJOR CHANGE: Cap raised to $225 (presumed reasonable) effective July 11, 2024. Fees over $225 require OCCC notification and cost analysis. Previously ~$150.
TX.verificationMethod: Primary: Texas Administrative Code §84.205, OCCC rule adoption document

UT.name: Utah
UT.vehicleTaxNotes: 4.7% state + up to 4% local. Varies significantly: Carbon County 6.35%, Park City 9.05%.
UT.registrationFee: ~$1,541 average for new vehicle (includes all fees); weight-based
UT.specialNotes: Title fee included in registration. Very high total registration costs.
UT.verificationMethod: Secondary: Utah DMV registration fees + state tax commission rates

VT.name: Vermont
VT.vehicleTaxNotes: 6% state + optional 1% local in some municipalities (Burlington, Hartford, Middlebury effective July 1, 2025).
VT.registrationFee: $76/year or $140/2-year + $2 Clean Air + EV fees ($89 BEV, $44.50 PHEV)
VT.specialNotes: Doc fees are taxable per Vermont DMV.
VT.verificationMethod: Primary: Vermont DMV fee schedule + registration page

VA.name: Virginia
VA.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Virginia does NOT allow trade-in deduction (exception: zero/near-zero emission vehicles).
VA.specialNotes: One of few states that does not allow trade-in tax credit. High doc fees with no cap.
VA.verificationMethod: Primary: Virginia DMV fee calculator + tax.virginia.gov

WA.name: Washington
WA.tradeInTaxCreditNotes: Trade-in property of like kind excluded from retail sales tax measure.
WA.specialNotes: Cap $200 effective July 1, 2022. Dealers cannot represent fee as required by law. Title fee increased to $6.50 effective Jan 1, 2026.

WV.name: West Virginia
WV.vehicleTaxNotes: 6% state + 1% local max. Charleston, Huntington, Morgantown at 7%.
WV.specialNotes: $575 cap set by DMV Dealer Advisory Board effective July 1, 2024 (increased from $499). CPI-adjusted annually starting July 1, 2025. Per WV Code 17A-6A-8A.
WV.verificationMethod: Secondary: WVADA News confirmed $575 cap (July 2024). Statute 17A-6A-8A. Needs primary verification.

WI.name: Wisconsin
WI.vehicleTaxNotes: 5% state + county/city additions. Milwaukee highest at 7.9%. 68 counties at 5.5%, 4 counties at 5%.
WI.specialNotes: Title fee increased from $164.50 to $214.50 effective Oct 1, 2025.
WI.verificationMethod: Primary: Wisconsin DOT fee schedule + DOR rate tables

WY.name: Wyoming
WY.vehicleTaxNotes: 4% state + 0-2% local. Teton Village/Alta at 9%. Cheyenne 6%, Jackson 6%.
WY.registrationFee: $30 state + county fee (3% of factory price x depreciation rate)
WY.verificationMethod: Secondary: Wyoming license plate org + Avalara rates

DC.name: District of Columbia
DC.vehicleTaxNotes: Weight-based excise tax: <3,500 lbs 6% (rising to 7% Oct 2026); 3,500-4,999 lbs 7% (rising to 8%); 5,000+ lbs 8% (rising to 9%). Hybrids reaching 40mpg exempt.
DC.specialNotes: Tax rates increasing Oct 1, 2025 and again Oct 1, 2026. EV exemption removed Oct 1, 2024. 2.5% credit card service fee effective Dec 15, 2025.
DC.verificationMethod: Primary: DC DMV fee estimator + DC tax handbook

statesWithDocFeeCap.notes: Increased from ~$500 via SB 362 effective July 1, 2024
$280 OR 5% of vehicle price, whichever is less
$387 OR 10% of cash price, whichever is lower
Applies to vehicles $10K+. Prohibited on vehicles under $10K.
Set by Dealer Advisory Board July 1, 2024. CPI-adjusted starting July 1, 2025.
